preNhl.note: The pre-NHL production record. Scoring is NOT comparable across leagues or to the NHL -- this is the biography, not a translation.

assetValue.flag: RFA at expiry: team control extends past the contract -- trade value understated; replacement-tier: price is the ledger's median small-deal cost

tradeComps.note: Real trades of players closest to him by PRODUCTION (points rank among same-season peers), age, contract AND years of control — so a controlled scorer matches other controlled scorers, not rentals. The return is only what his old team got back FROM the team that landed him; “package” means he wasn't the only piece going out, so that return isn't his alone. For the genuinely elite — who almost never trade, so their only comps are distressed sales — we show a MODELED estimate of what a player his caliber would command instead.

fairContract.note: Fair AAV = average projected annual market value over the term (aging-curve WAR walk x $4.71M/WAR, each year floored at the league minimum). Longer terms average in decline years, so the fair AAV falls. Real deals carry a term/security premium this does not model -- it is the production-fair anchor, not a prediction of the signing.

longTerm.boundNote: Players at his production almost never get 5+yr offers — ~9% of young RFAs below ~45 pts/82 sign long (two-thirds bridge). If a team did offer term today it would price near the bridge, not below it. His market re-prices nightly as the sample grows — a strong season changes this read.

value.summary: His total on-ice value, converted to WINS (WAR). It adds up everything he does — even-strength play, the power play and penalty kill, his penalty discipline RELATIVE TO HIS POSITION (defensemen structurally take more than they draw, so only the differential vs other D counts), and faceoffs — and the grade ranks that total against other players at his position over the last three seasons.
value.method: Total WAR sums these four, each priced in wins. The grade is this player's percentile vs same-position peers over the 3-season window.

overall.summary: His two-way impact at 5-on-5: do his team's scoring chances go UP, and the other team's go DOWN, when he's on the ice? We isolate his effect from his linemates, the competition, and where his shifts start — so it's his fingerprint on the play, not just his teammates' work.
overall.method: Teammate-, competition- and zone-start-adjusted 5v5 expected goals, via ridge regression over shift stints. An impact estimate, not a box score.

offense.summary: His effect on the chances his team CREATES at 5-on-5 — how much shot-quality (expected goals) his line generates with him out there, after adjusting for who he plays with and against.
offense.method: His share of 5v5 expected-goals creation, adjusted for who he plays with and against.

defense.summary: His effect on the chances his team ALLOWS at 5-on-5 — how well shot-quality against is held down with him on the ice, after adjusting for teammates and competition. A higher grade means fewer good chances against.
defense.method: His suppression of 5v5 expected goals against, teammate- and competition-adjusted. Higher means fewer chances allowed while he's on the ice.

finishing.summary: Whether he converts MORE than an average shooter at his position would on the same shots — with each shot priced by how league shooters actually convert its CONTEXT (danger band × strength state × rebound), not just its geometry. Trusted more the more he's shot; the band says how sure we are.
finishing.method: Goals minus cell-expected goals per 100 shots, centered within position, then shrunk toward a shot-speed-informed prior by sample size (younger samples shrink harder). Cells are fit walk-forward — a season is priced only by the seasons before it. This is the same finishing number the WAR stack uses.

hits60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
hits60.label: Physicality
hits60.summary: Physicality is how often he throws a body check — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 159 hits over his last 86 games (11.9 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other forwards.
hits60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

blocks60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
blocks60.label: Shot blocking
blocks60.summary: Shot blocking is how willing he is to get in a shooting lane — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 56 blocked shots over his last 86 games (4.2 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other forwards.
blocks60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

takeaways60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
takeaways60.label: Puck stripping
takeaways60.summary: Puck stripping is how often he strips the puck off an opponent — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 12 takeaways over his last 86 games (0.9 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other forwards.
takeaways60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

giveaways60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
giveaways60.label: Puck security
giveaways60.summary: Puck security is how often he turns the puck over (fewer is better) — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 37 giveaways over his last 86 games (2.8 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other forwards.
giveaways60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

primary_assists60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
primary_assists60.label: Playmaking
primary_assists60.summary: Playmaking is how often he makes the primary pass on a goal — 0.37 per 60 minutes of ice time over his last 3 seasons, ranked against other forwards.

rush_xg60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
rush_xg60.label: Rush offense
rush_xg60.summary: Rush offense is the danger of the chances he creates off the rush — 0.11 per 60 minutes of ice time over his last 3 seasons, ranked against other forwards.

skills.window: recent 3 seasons, TOI-weighted, vs position peers
skills.note: hits/takeaways/giveaways are RTSS-tracked (arena scorer bias, not adjusted); giveaways shown as puck security (100 = fewest). All-situations TOI denominates rates.

edge.note: NHL EDGE puck-and-player tracking; percentiles are league-wide for the season. Tracking starts 2021-22.

shot.note: Raw puck speed (NHL EDGE) -- a physical tool, not a finishing grade.

context.note: From shift overlaps. Headline is usage (ice time of the players around him); production is the model's grade of those players. A big gap means leaned-on help that grades below its minutes.

meta.war: Total WAR = EV (5v5 RAPM) + special teams + penalties + faceoffs + finishing. xG-based play-driving plus shrunk shooting talent; single-season RAPM is noisy -- the pooled multi-year RAPM is the stable read.
meta.rapm: off/def/total RAPM are net xG per 60 at 5v5, teammate- and competition-adjusted (ridge regression over shift stints).
meta.coverage: On-ice metrics cover seasons with shift data: 2010-11 to present (100% of regular-season games).

_explain.summary: Adam Edstrom's -0.08 total WAR in 2025-26 ranks in the 25th percentile of NHL skaters. It breaks down as: even-strength play (5v5): +0.01 WAR (25th percentile); special teams (PP/PK): +0.00 WAR (37th percentile); finishing (shooting talent): -0.09 WAR (32nd percentile); penalty discipline (vs position): +0.01 WAR (52nd percentile); faceoffs: -0.01 WAR (24th percentile).
_explain.narrative: Adam Edstrom's -0.08 WAR in 2025-26 is driven most by even-strength play (5v5) (+0.01, 25th percentile) -- within 5v5 he tilts toward offense (offense -0.04/60, 41st percentile; defense -0.10/60, 22nd percentile). His weakest area is finishing (shooting talent) (-0.09, 32nd percentile). (xG-based; single-season RAPM is noisy -- see multi-year RAPM for the stable read.)
